---
title: "4.2 Introduction to Using Data Sets"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Data sets need algorithms

A <span class="term">data set</span> (数据集) is a collection of related data values.

In AP CSA, a data set is often processed with:

::: {.tight-list}
- arrays
- `ArrayList`
- text files
- loops
- selection logic
:::

## Algorithm Task

### Average GPA pseudocode

Reorder the algorithm for computing an average.

```text
Initialize total to 0.
Initialize count to 0.
For each GPA in the data set:
    Add the GPA to total.
    Add 1 to count.
Divide total by count.
Return or print the average.
```

This is the same accumulator pattern from Unit 2 applied to a data collection.

## Honor Roll Prompt

### Filter data by a property

Write pseudocode for printing all students with a GPA at least `3.5`.

```text
For each student in the data set:
    If the student's GPA is at least 3.5:
        Print the student's name.
```

Class focus: filtering is selection inside traversal.

## Data Set Operations

### Common classroom algorithms

::: {.tight-list}
- count how many items match a condition
- compute sum or average
- find a minimum or maximum
- search for a target item
- build a new collection from selected items
:::

The rest of Unit 4 gives Java structures for implementing these operations.

## Quick Check

### Match task to pattern

| Task | Pattern |
|---|---|
| total all GPAs | accumulator |
| print all honor roll students | filter |
| find highest score | max |
| find a named student | search |

Use the task verb to choose the algorithm pattern.

## Classroom Check

### A strong answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain that a data set is a collection of related values
- connect data set processing to traversal with loops
- use an accumulator for averages
- use selection inside a loop to filter data
- identify common data set tasks such as count, search, min, max, and average
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
